sections.paragraph:
πίμπλημι [cp. πλῆθος] ‘cause to be in a condition that allows for no further addition’, fill

—a. of physical things Mt 22:10; 27:48; Lk 5:7; of non-physical things: relating to feelings or emotions 4:28; 5:26; 6:11; Ac 3:10; 5:17; 13:45; 19:29; relating to the Holy Spirit Lk 1:15, 41, 67; Ac 2:4; 4:8, 31; 9:17; 13:9.

—b. of a point in time that marks the fulfillment of a scheduled moment or expectation for someth. to take place, come to an end Lk 1:23, 57; 2:6, 21f; come to fruition 21:22.
